quoteTime: thirty-two minutes past eleven A. M.
quoteLast: , we     engaged the sloop-of-war, having first received her fire at     the distance of fifty or sixty yards, which space we gradually     lessened until we laid her on board, after a well-supported     fire of forty-three minutes; and although so near, while     loading our last broadside, that our rammers were shoved     against the side of the enemy, our men exhibited the same     alacrity which they had done during the whole of the action.
